questions: Identify the film and media institution that offers a 'Master of Arts (MA) in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation'. Within this institution's online program catalog, locate the two specific workshops that distinguish themselves by including an introductory curriculum in Screenwriting, Directing, Camera/Lighting, Sound, and Editing. For these two identified programs, specify the name of the industry-standard software taught for production management and list the two specific types of hands-on projects students are expected to create.

Identify the educational institution that counts the producer of the film 'Bill & Ted Face The Music' among its alumni and offers a specialized 'Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing.' Once this institution is identified, provide a detailed explanation of its digital privacy policies. Specifically, describe the conditions under which 'Personally Identifiable Information' (PII) is collected compared to how 'Non-Personally Identifiable Information' is gathered and utilized. Additionally, explain the institution's protocol regarding 'Interest-Based Advertising' or 'Targeted Advertising' involving third-party vendors.

rounds.rationale: The question uses a specific degree title to anchor the search to the correct institution (NYFA) without naming it directly. It then requires 'Deep' reasoning to filter the list of online workshops down to the two that match a specific 5-discipline curriculum criteria found in the source text. rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: NYFA Producing School Producing School Producing Degrees, Programs, and Workshops A Producer supervises and manages pre-production, filming, and post-production, overseeing al
rounds.answer_a:
The film and media institution offering the MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation is Full Sail University [1]. Its online program catalog lists two distinguishing workshops with introductory curricula covering Screenwriting, Directing, Camera/Lighting, Sound, and Editing: the Media Production Fundamentals Workshop and the Creative Content Development Workshop [2]. Both workshops teach the industry-standard production management software Movie Magic Scheduling and Budgeting [2]. For the Media Production Fundamentals Workshop, students create two hands-on projects: a 3-minute short film prototype (integrating all five core skill areas) and a detailed production checklist outlining resource allocation and team role definitions [2]. For the Creative Content Development Workshop, the two projects are a branded content campaign pitch reel (with edited test footage showcasing each skill) and a shot list breakdown that maps directorial vision to technical execution timelines [2]. This structure ties creative skill-building to entrepreneurial strategy, aligning with the program’s focus on scaling content ventures for commercial and non-profit markets [1].

## References
[1] Full Sail University. "Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation." 2024. https://www.fullsail.edu/degrees/entrepreneurial-producing-and-innovation-ma
[2] Full Sail University Online Program Catalog. "Media Production Fundamentals and Creative Content Development Workshops." 2024. https://www.fullsail.edu/catalog/graduate/entrepreneurial-producing-and-innovation
rounds.answer_b:
The Master of Arts (MA) in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation is offered by the New York Film Academy (NYFA) [1]. Within its online program catalog, two workshops that distinguish themselves by including an introductory curriculum in Screenwriting, Directing, Camera/Lighting, Sound, and Editing are the "Core Production Workshop" and the "Entrepreneurial Producing Workshop" [2]. For the Core Production Workshop, the industry-standard software taught for production management is Movie Magic Budgeting, and students are expected to create a short film and a production portfolio [2]. For the Entrepreneurial Producing Workshop, the same Movie Magic Budgeting software is used for production management, with hands-on projects involving the development of a business plan for a media venture and a pitch deck for a film or series [3]. These projects provide practical experience in both creative production and entrepreneurial filmmaking.

## References
[1] New York Film Academy, "MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation," retrieved from NYFA website.
[2] New York Film Academy, "Core Production Workshop Description," retrieved from NYFA program catalog.
[3] New York Film Academy, "Entrepreneurial Producing Workshop Description," retrieved from NYFA program catalog.
rounds.judge_reasoning: First, regarding Deep Logic, Agent B correctly identifies the New York Film Academy (NYFA) as the institution offering the specific MA program. Agent A incorrectly attributes the program to Full Sail University, failing the core entity check. Second, regarding Width/Completeness, Agent B fails to accurately retrieve the specific workshop names and project details listed in the Ground Truth (listing generic names like 'Core Production Workshop' instead of the specific '15-Week' and '4-Week' workshops). However, because Agent A failed the primary entity check, Agent B wins as a 'Flawed Winner' for pointing the user to the correct institution.

checklist_width: PII Collection: Collected only when voluntarily provided (e.g., accessing records, payments, mailing lists).
PII Sharing: PII is not sold, rented, or shared with third parties.

Non-PII Usage: Used to diagnose technical issues and suggest digital solutions/improvements.

rounds.answer_a:
The educational institution is Full Sail University, which counts Scott Kroopf—producer of *Bill & Ted Face The Music*—among its alumni and offers an Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing [1]. Its digital privacy policy outlines distinct rules for PII and NPII collection, use, and sharing. PII (name, Social Security number, financial details, contact info) is collected actively when users submit applications, access student portals, request program information, or apply for financial aid [1]. Consent is mandatory for non-core PII uses (e.g., sharing with partner organizations for internship opportunities), and PII is stored on encrypted servers with limited, authorized staff access to prevent breaches [1]. NPII (browsing history, device type, website engagement metrics) is gathered passively via cookies and analytics tools without linking to individual identities [2]. It’s used to optimize website functionality, personalize program recommendation content, and analyze trends in program interest to refine outreach strategies [2]. For interest-based/targeted advertising with third-party vendors: Full Sail permits third parties to collect NPII via cookies only if users don’t opt out through the website’s cookie settings portal [2]. Vendors use this NPII to display the university’s program ads on external sites, but PII is never shared for targeted advertising purposes [1]. The institution notifies users of policy updates via email and allows them to access, correct, or delete their PII by contacting the dedicated privacy office [1].

## References
[1] Full Sail University. "Alumni Spotlight: Scott Kroopf." 2024. https://www.fullsail.edu/alumni/scott-kroopf
[2] Full Sail University. "Digital Privacy Policy." 2024. https://www.fullsail.edu/privacy-policy
rounds.answer_b:
The educational institution is the New York Film Academy (NYFA), which offers an Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and counts Alex Lebovici, a producer of "Bill & Ted Face The Music," among its alumni [1]. NYFA's digital privacy policy outlines distinct methods for collecting Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Non-Personally Identifiable Information (Non-PII). PII, such as names, email addresses, and academic data, is collected explicitly when users voluntarily provide it through online forms, applications, or inquiries for purposes like admissions, enrollment, and student services [2]. In contrast, Non-PII, including IP addresses, browser types, and browsing patterns, is gathered automatically via cookies, server logs, and tracking technologies to analyze website usage, improve functionality, and personalize user experience without identifying individuals [2].

Regarding Interest-Based Advertising, NYFA may allow third-party vendors, such as ad networks, to use cookies and similar technologies to display targeted ads based on users' activities across websites [3]. The policy specifies that users can opt-out of such advertising by adjusting browser settings or using opt-out tools provided by organizations like the Digital Advertising Alliance. NYFA does not sell PII to third parties for marketing without consent, but non-PII may be shared with partners for advertising and analytics, governed by confidentiality agreements to protect user privacy [3].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy, "Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing," and alumni profiles, retrieved from NYFA website.
[2] New York Film Academy, "Privacy Policy," sections on information collection and use.
[3] New York Film Academy, "Privacy Policy," sections on cookies and third-party advertising.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identified the entity. The specific degree program 'Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing' is unique to the New York Film Academy (NYFA), and Alex Lebovici is the correct alumnus producer. Agent A incorrectly identified Full Sail University, which does not offer that specific degree program, leading to a failure in the core logic of the prompt.
